jobs.title: Entry to Mid-Level Environmental Scientist / GIS Technician Analyst
jobs.description:
McLane Environmental, LLC is currently seeking candidates for an entry- to mid-level Environmental Scientist / GIS Analyst position for our Princeton, New Jersey office. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to work as a part of a collegial and highly talented team on diverse ground water-related projects, including water supply issues, wastewater disposal, contaminant transport, site remediation, saltwater intrusion, and environmental dispute resolution.

The candidate should have a strong ability to compile, manage, interpret, and present environmental data. The position requires a BS degree in environmental science, engineering, geoscience or related discipline; experience with GIS software; experience with data management and graphics software; a working knowledge of geology and hydrogeology; and strong quantitative and communication skills. The successful candidate should have experience in analyzing spatial data and generating maps using GIS; conducting basic internet information searches; the ability to compile, organize, synthesize and perform simple analyses of environmental data using standard software; and should function well in a team environment. Experience with website development and programming is considered a plus.

Responsibilities for the Environmental Scientist position include, but are not limited to:

Compiling and analyzing spatial data in GIS, and generating maps and figures

Review and synthesis of hydrogeologic studies, environmental reports, and other technical documents and information

Conducting project-related research

Compilation and review of environmental datasets

Assisting senior staff with a range of tasks, including 2D and 3D data visualization and analysis, analysis of aquifer and groundwater quality data, and groundwater modeling

Preparation of technical reports, graphics and client presentations

Assistance with business development and marketing activities

At McLane Environmental, we provide strategic environmental services and quantitative groundwater project support to corporate and government clients and law firms throughout the United States. We offer a competitive salary and full benefits package. The Princeton area is great for work, family, and recreation, and is close to Philadelphia, New York City, and many outdoor recreation areas. For more information on our company and the Princeton area, visit our website at www.McLaneEnv.com. U.S. Citizen, LPR I-155 card holder, TN visa-eligible only.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $45,000.00 - $75,000.00 per year

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Jobright is a next-generation AI job search platform built to make career navigation faster, smarter, and more personal. They are looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw data into actionable intelligence that shapes how our agents learn, adapt, and perform.

Why Join Us

Work directly on AI systems that are actively changing how people find careers
Your analysis will influence real product decisions, not just sit in dashboards
Collaborate across product, engineering, and client teams in a high-ownership environment
Be part of a team that takes data seriously as a driver of agent improvement

Responsibilities

Embed directly into client-facing and internal workflows, ensuring data-driven insights are woven into the decision-making processes of our AI agents
Design and deliver custom data reports and visualizations that surface clear, expert-level findings for both product teams and external stakeholders
Field data requests with speed and precision, translating complex analytical outputs into accessible, well-communicated narratives
Collaborate regularly with your manager and cross-functional teammates to continuously refine data models and track agent performance over time

Qualifications

Required

Recent grad or early-career professional with 0–2 years of experience in data analysis or a related technical field
Strong communicator with the ability to present data trends clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Solid grounding in statistics, analytical methodologies, and the underlying logic of AI and machine learning systems
Must be based in and authorized to work in the United States; occasional travel for team summits may be required

Preferred

Prior internship or project experience in a data analyst, research, or support role within a tech or AI-focused organization
Demonstrated ability to extract practical, digestible insights from large and complex datasets
Technical proficiency in Python or R, data modeling concepts, and SQL-based database management

jobs.title: Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)
jobs.description:
Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)

Role Description Support business and operational decision-making by collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data from multiple sources to generate actionable insights and recommendations. Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that enable stakeholders to monitor business performance, identify trends, and track key performance indicators. Perform data validation, cleansing, and quality assurance activities to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of datasets. Analyze large datasets to uncover patterns, opportunities, risks, and areas for improvement, and communicate findings through clear reports and presentations. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support forecasting, performance measurement, process optimization, and strategic initiatives that contribute to organizational growth and operational excellence.
Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Business Administration, Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field. Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail. Proficiency in spreadsheets, data analysis tools, reporting platforms, and data visualization software. Familiarity with SQL, database concepts, statistical methodologies, and data management practices is advantageous. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear and actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong organizational and time-management capabilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively. Knowledge of business intelligence, reporting frameworks, data governance, forecasting, performance measurement, and process improvement principles is highly beneficial.

jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Company Description
Home Partners of America® is a residential real estate company that provides accessible paths to homeownership for residents across the United States. The organization focuses on offering flexible lease and home purchase options that can help individuals and households find quality homes in desirable communities. By combining real estate expertise with data-driven decision-making, Home Partners of America® aims to create long-term value for residents, investors, and partners. Team members collaborate in a mission-driven environment that values integrity, transparency, and innovation. The company supports remote work and strives to foster an inclusive, equitable workplace for professionals from diverse backgrounds.
Role Description
This is a part-time, remote Senior Data Analyst role responsible for turning complex data into clear insights that support strategic and operational decisions. The Senior Data Analyst will gather, clean, and analyze data from multiple sources, build reports and dashboards, and deliver concise findings to stakeholders across the business. Day-to-day tasks include designing and executing analyses on resident behavior, portfolio performance, and market trends, using statistical methods and data modeling techniques to identify patterns and opportunities. The person in this role will partner closely with product, operations, finance, and leadership teams to define metrics, track key performance indicators, and recommend data-informed actions. Additional responsibilities include documenting methodologies, ensuring data quality and consistency, and mentoring colleagues on best practices in analytics and data literacy.
Qualifications

Strong Analytical Skills and experience with Data Analytics, including the ability to interpret complex datasets and translate them into actionable business insights.
Solid foundation in Statistics and quantitative methods for hypothesis testing, forecasting, and performance measurement.
Hands-on experience with Data Modeling, including designing, maintaining, and optimizing data structures to support reporting and analysis.
Clear and effective Communication skills, with the ability to present findings to technical and non-technical audiences in a structured, concise manner.
Proficiency with analytics and data tools (e.g., SQL, Excel, BI platforms such as Power BI, Tableau, or Looker; experience with Python or R is a plus).
Demonstrated experience in a data-focused role, preferably in real estate, financial services, or a similar data-rich environment.
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related discipline; advanced degree is a plus.
Ability to work independently in a remote environment, manage multiple priorities, and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
Commitment to ethical use of data, attention to detail, and a continuous improvement mindset.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Part-time

Job function

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
About Our Client:

The organization operates as a market research and analytics firm specializing in the disruptive economy. It addresses challenges in analyzing vast alternative data points to provide actionable insights across sectors such as Consumer Goods, AI & Software, Cloud, and Payments. Serving top investment funds and large corporations globally, the company supports high-stakes decision-making through proprietary technology and substantial data analysis. It maintains a presence in the US, APAC, and India, with a culture recognized for transparency, ownership, and continuous mastery.

About the Opportunity:

The **Data Analyst** role involves owning and managing data products within sector teams focused on industries like consumer goods, software, or financial services. The position is responsible for ensuring data accuracy, improving methodologies, and delivering trusted insights that help investors understand company performance and market trends. This role contributes by tackling complex analytical problems, collaborating cross-functionally, and continuously enhancing product value and team processes.

Responsibilities:

• Own data products end-to-end, ensuring accuracy, client value, and operational efficiency
• Develop expertise in proprietary datasets and methodologies
• Address complex data challenges with analytical judgment and creativity
• Utilize AI and automation tools to improve workflow speed and quality
• Collaborate with Research, Engineering, and other teams to align practices and unblock work
• Identify and implement improvements to products and processes

Requirements:

• 3-5+ years of experience in data analytics or related fields involving complex data
• Proficiency in SQL; experience with Python or PySpark is advantageous
• Proven ability to learn complex data methodologies and build strong mental models
• Demonstrated initiative in problem-solving and ownership beyond assigned scope
• Effective communication skills for technical and non-technical audiences
• Active use and enthusiasm for leveraging AI tools to enhance work processes

Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

Benefits & Perks:

• Flexible work hours and vacation
• Generous 401(k) match
• Parental leave
• Team events and wellness budget
• Learning reimbursement

jobs.description:

This organization operates within the project support and coordination space, focusing on ensuring accuracy and alignment between contracts, customer projects, and estimates. It addresses challenges related to discrepancies in quantities, materials, and labor by maintaining clear communication channels between field staff, subcontractors, and retail partners. The company supports efficient project progression through detailed review and issue resolution.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Project Coordinator is responsible for reviewing customer projects and work orders to ensure they align with contract expectations. This role involves identifying and correcting discrepancies, facilitating communication among stakeholders, and supporting the smooth advancement of projects. The position contributes to maintaining service quality and operational efficiency by providing critical oversight and support to project teams.

• Correct discrepancies in quantities, materials, or labor
• Log support requests and communicate necessary changes to retail partners
• Assist team members with inquiries regarding reviewed projects
• Maintain accurate records in CIO and IMS systems
• Communicate effectively with field staff, subcontractors, and retail personnel
• Respond promptly to voicemails, emails, and texts during work hours
• Provide backup support to operations and administrative teams

4. Requirements:

• Capability to handle multiple forms of communication promptly

jobs.title: Client Services Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The organization operates within the energy transformation industry, addressing the challenges utilities face as they navigate complex, interconnected demands such as load flexibility, electrification, and carbon reduction. Focused on energy efficiency and clean energy solutions, this women-led firm supports utilities in managing these transitions effectively, aiming to make a measurable impact on energy practices.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Sr. Rebate Processing Specialist plays a critical role in the rebate processing operations by ensuring accurate data management, coordinating project activities, and supporting the delivery of program objectives. This position contributes to maintaining data integrity and smooth workflow processes that are essential for the successful execution of energy efficiency programs.

3. Responsibilities:
• Process rebate applications and related documentation following program guidelines and service level agreements.
• Perform timely and accurate data entry, validation, and maintenance across various systems.
• Coordinate project activities, deliverables, timelines, and documentation to support program execution.
• Review applications and documentation for accuracy, completeness, and compliance.
• Prepare and maintain reports and project documentation to support internal teams and client needs.
• Collaborate with stakeholders to resolve data discrepancies and process issues.
• Support program launches, system updates, and operational changes through testing and documentation.
• Identify and recommend process improvements for efficiency and accuracy.
• Maintain records and audit documentation per program and client standards.
• Assist with special projects, operational initiatives, and administrative tasks as needed.

4. Requirements:
• High school diploma or equivalent; associate or bachelor’s degree preferred.
• Minimum 2 years experience in data entry, project coordination, program administration, or operations support.
• Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
• Excellent organizational and time management skills.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Office, including Excel, Word, and Outlook.
• Experience with databases, CRM systems, or data management platforms preferred.
• Ability to analyze information, identify discrepancies, and resolve issues efficiently.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills.
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
• Experience in rebate processing, energy efficiency programs, utilities, or regulated environments is a plus.
• Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information appropriately.

5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:
• Base salary range of $18 to $20 per hour, with potential eligibility for a discretionary annual bonus.

• Three weeks of paid vacation annually
• Paid holidays

jobs.title: Project Coordinator 1 (41-5004) Anchorage Therapeutic Courts
jobs.description:
Job Description

The Alaska Court System Therapeutic Courts, are looking to hire a Project Coordinator 1 to support both the Anchorage Felony Wellness Court and the Anchorage Municipal Wellness Court. This position is responsible for coordinating the daily operations and administrative functions of these therapeutic court programs. This position serves as a liaison between the court, treatment providers, probation, attorneys, and community partners to ensure effective communication and program coordination. This position works under the direction of the Deputy Director of Therapeutic Courts.

Employees transferring from another State of Alaska agency may receive consideration for an advance step placement.

Regular Job Duties Will Include

PROJECT ADMINISTRATION

Coordinate daily operations of the therapeutic court; meet with judges and other team members to ensure the smooth operation of the court processes;
Provide judicial officers information necessary to ensure the efficient and effective operations of court pre-meetings and court hearings;
Serve as primary contact in the court system for referrals;
Flag eligible cases, track incoming applicants/participants, monitor collection of collateral information, and track timelines of team decisions;
Process, approve or deny applications for Flexible Funds for court participants.

PROJECT CONSISTENCY

Work with the Director of Therapeutic Courts and other project coordinators to improve program effectiveness, consistency and uniformity with the goal of making it a strong and efficient part of the ongoing court/governmental system;
Work with court team to create and modify forms that will ensure consistency with court policies, procedures, and data collection needs.

PROJECT DEVELOPMENT

Work with various team members and partner agencies to update and revise policy and procedural manuals and other court documents as needed for the court;
Assist in the development of program evaluation criteria;
Work with the Director of Therapeutic Courts to develop and update training plans to support the needs of the court;
Facilitate administrative meetings which includes scheduling, preparing agendas and documents to be reviewed, taking notes, and distributing minutes.

COLLABORATION

Work on community and statewide multi-program needs (e.g., drug testing protocols, housing, vocational training and work placement) in consultation with court administrators and the Director of Therapeutic Courts;
Meet with partnering agencies to improve day-to-day operations;
Attend regular staff meetings and monthly statewide project coordinator teleconferences;
Participate in statewide planning and discussion with other project coordinators as scheduled by the Director of Therapeutic Courts.

OUTREACH AND EDUCATION

Write informational materials for public distribution;
With supervisory approval, perform community education and outreach by making regular presentations to community entities.

This position may be flexibly-staffed. An incumbent must have worked as a Project Coordinator 1 for at least two (2) years. Once that requirement is met, progression to the higher level may occur when the incumbent: 1) successfully completes a specified training plan; 2) meets all other specified terms of the flexible staffing agreement; 3) meets the minimum qualifications of the higher level; 4) is certified by the supervisor as capable to perform at the higher level; and 5) is approved by the Human Resources Department.

When certified, the position may be reclassified to Project Coordinator 2, salary range 18 ($3,171.75 bi-weekly).

This position is in the partially exempt service and the incumbent serves "at-will" to the hiring authority. This position is exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act and is not eligible for overtime compensation.

The Work Hours

Full-time court employees are currently scheduled to work 37.5 hours per week. The work hours for this position are Monday – Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. with a one-hour lunch break.

Exceptional Benefits For Full-Time Employees

We offer a competitive benefits package that includes 12 paid holidays
, medical, dental, and optional vision coverage
, and employer-paid life insurance
. Employees are enrolled in the Public Employees Retirement System (PERS)
and the Alaska Supplemental Annuity Plan (SBS)
in lieu of contributions to Social Security, with access to the Alaska Deferred Compensation Plan
. Personal leave
is accrued for vacation, illness, or personal needs.

Enjoy peace of mind and long-term security while serving your community.

See
http://doa.alaska.gov/drb/index.html
for additional information.

IMPORTANT: Please attach a letter of interest in employment as a Project Coordinator 1 with the Alaska Court System. Failure to submit a letter of interest may disqualify you from consideration for an interview.

For more information on the Alaska Court System, please visit:
http://www.courts.alaska.gov

For information about what state courts do, please watch the following short video:
VIDEO: The who, what, when, where and how of state courts

The Alaska Court System is an EEO employer and proudly promotes diversity.

Minimum Qualifications

A bachelor's degree from an accredited college AND two years of professional or administrative experience in mental health, community development, research, the behavioral sciences, the social sciences, public administration, or a closely related field.

OR

Six years of professional or administrative experience in mental health, community development, research, the behavioral sciences, the social sciences, public administration, or a closely related field.

OR

Any combination totaling six years of education from an accredited college AND professional or administrative experience in mental health, community development, research, the behavioral sciences, the social sciences, public administration, or a closely related field.

Additional Required Information

This applies to your application submission.

Documents To Be Attached To The Application (REQUIRED)

A letter of interest, completed per the instructions below.
Post-secondary transcripts, if using education to meet minimum qualifications.
A list of three (3) professional references who have had supervisory authority over you, including your most recent supervisor, along with their daytime contact phone numbers and email addresses.

LETTER OF INTEREST
Please attach a professional letter of interest to your application before submitting it online or, if unable to attach to your application, email it to recruitment@akcourts.gov
.

Your Letter Of Interest Should

Be professionally written and include proper format, grammar, spelling, and punctuation.
Explain how you meet the minimum qualifications of the position.
Express your interest in employment with the Alaska Court System.

The letter will be used to help determine which applicants will advance to the interview phase of the recruitment and selection process.

LEGAL EMPLOYMENT

The State’s online recruitment system, Workplace Alaska, requires applicants to certify that they have a legal right to accept employment in the United States. It is the responsibility of the employee to maintain the appropriate documentation to accept or continue legal employment. The Alaska Court System does not sponsor visas.

Work Experience

If using work experience not already documented in your application, provide the employer’s name, your job title, dates of employment and whether full or part-time. Applications will be reviewed to determine if the responses are supported and Minimum Qualifications are clearly met. If they are not, the applicant will not advance to the interview and selection phase of the recruitment.

EDUCATION

If post-secondary education is required to meet the Minimum Qualifications, you must fill in the Education and Training section of the application. If you have not obtained a degree, please indicate the number of credit hours earned in the Degree Obtained field. Copies of transcripts are required to verify educational credentials used to meet or support the minimum qualifications for the position and are required with each application.

SPECIAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR FOREIGN EDUCATION

Education completed in foreign colleges or universities may be used to meet the minimum qualifications listed above. If utilizing this education, you must show that the education credentials have been submitted to a private organization that specializes in the interpretation of foreign educational credentials and that such education has been deemed to be at least equivalent to that gained in conventional U.S. education programs; or an accredited U.S. post-secondary institution reports the other institution as one whose transcript is given full value, or full value is given in subject areas applicable to the curricula at the post-secondary institution. It is your responsibility to provide such evidence when applying.

SELECTION PROCEDURE

Applicants will be required to meet the Minimum Qualifications as outlined above. Unfortunately, we cannot always interview all applicants. If your application is considered "not qualified," you will be sent a notice within two weeks after the closing date for this recruitment. If you believe your application was improperly disqualified, you may appeal this decision to the Human Resources Director at 820 West 4th Avenue, Anchorage, AK 99501-2005 within five workdays from the date the notice is mailed to you. Qualified applications will be forwarded to the hiring supervisor .

MULTIPLE VACANCIES NOTICE

This recruitment may be used for more than one (1) vacancy. The applicant pool acquired during this recruitment may be used for future vacancies for up to sixty (60) days after this recruitment closes. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply to each recruitment notice to ensure consideration for all vacancies.

NEPOTISM REVIEW

To avoid a conflict of interest and the appearance of impropriety, applicants who have a spouse, close relative, and/or regular member of their household employed by the Alaska Court System may not be considered for employment without the approval of the Administrative Director .

BACKGROUND CHECK

A background check will be conducted prior to an employment offer being made. Applicants who have prior convictions that have been set aside under the terms of a suspended imposition of sentence (SIS) may answer "no" to these questions. If you are not sure if your situation requires disclosure, please contact the Human Resources Department at (907) 264-8242. The Court System reserves the right to confirm all background information.

Contact Information

Contact Title: Recruitment

E-Mail:
recruitment@akcourts.gov

Mailing Address: 820 West 4th Avenue, Anchorage, AK 99501

Phone Number: (907) 264-8242

new_jobs.title: Entry to Mid-Level Environmental Scientist / GIS Technician Analyst
new_jobs.description:
McLane Environmental, LLC is currently seeking candidates for an entry- to mid-level Environmental Scientist / GIS Analyst position for our Princeton, New Jersey office. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to work as a part of a collegial and highly talented team on diverse ground water-related projects, including water supply issues, wastewater disposal, contaminant transport, site remediation, saltwater intrusion, and environmental dispute resolution.

The candidate should have a strong ability to compile, manage, interpret, and present environmental data. The position requires a BS degree in environmental science, engineering, geoscience or related discipline; experience with GIS software; experience with data management and graphics software; a working knowledge of geology and hydrogeology; and strong quantitative and communication skills. The successful candidate should have experience in analyzing spatial data and generating maps using GIS; conducting basic internet information searches; the ability to compile, organize, synthesize and perform simple analyses of environmental data using standard software; and should function well in a team environment. Experience with website development and programming is considered a plus.

Responsibilities for the Environmental Scientist position include, but are not limited to:

Compiling and analyzing spatial data in GIS, and generating maps and figures

Review and synthesis of hydrogeologic studies, environmental reports, and other technical documents and information

Conducting project-related research

Compilation and review of environmental datasets

Assisting senior staff with a range of tasks, including 2D and 3D data visualization and analysis, analysis of aquifer and groundwater quality data, and groundwater modeling

Preparation of technical reports, graphics and client presentations

Assistance with business development and marketing activities

At McLane Environmental, we provide strategic environmental services and quantitative groundwater project support to corporate and government clients and law firms throughout the United States. We offer a competitive salary and full benefits package. The Princeton area is great for work, family, and recreation, and is close to Philadelphia, New York City, and many outdoor recreation areas. For more information on our company and the Princeton area, visit our website at www.McLaneEnv.com. U.S. Citizen, LPR I-155 card holder, TN visa-eligible only.

Jobright is a next-generation AI job search platform built to make career navigation faster, smarter, and more personal. They are looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw data into actionable intelligence that shapes how our agents learn, adapt, and perform.

Why Join Us

Work directly on AI systems that are actively changing how people find careers
Your analysis will influence real product decisions, not just sit in dashboards
Collaborate across product, engineering, and client teams in a high-ownership environment
Be part of a team that takes data seriously as a driver of agent improvement

Responsibilities

Embed directly into client-facing and internal workflows, ensuring data-driven insights are woven into the decision-making processes of our AI agents
Design and deliver custom data reports and visualizations that surface clear, expert-level findings for both product teams and external stakeholders
Field data requests with speed and precision, translating complex analytical outputs into accessible, well-communicated narratives
Collaborate regularly with your manager and cross-functional teammates to continuously refine data models and track agent performance over time

Qualifications

Required

Recent grad or early-career professional with 0–2 years of experience in data analysis or a related technical field
Strong communicator with the ability to present data trends clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Solid grounding in statistics, analytical methodologies, and the underlying logic of AI and machine learning systems
Must be based in and authorized to work in the United States; occasional travel for team summits may be required

Preferred

Prior internship or project experience in a data analyst, research, or support role within a tech or AI-focused organization
Demonstrated ability to extract practical, digestible insights from large and complex datasets
Technical proficiency in Python or R, data modeling concepts, and SQL-based database management

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)
new_jobs.description:
Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)

Role Description Support business and operational decision-making by collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data from multiple sources to generate actionable insights and recommendations. Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that enable stakeholders to monitor business performance, identify trends, and track key performance indicators. Perform data validation, cleansing, and quality assurance activities to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of datasets. Analyze large datasets to uncover patterns, opportunities, risks, and areas for improvement, and communicate findings through clear reports and presentations. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support forecasting, performance measurement, process optimization, and strategic initiatives that contribute to organizational growth and operational excellence.
Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Business Administration, Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field. Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail. Proficiency in spreadsheets, data analysis tools, reporting platforms, and data visualization software. Familiarity with SQL, database concepts, statistical methodologies, and data management practices is advantageous. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear and actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong organizational and time-management capabilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively. Knowledge of business intelligence, reporting frameworks, data governance, forecasting, performance measurement, and process improvement principles is highly beneficial.

new_jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
Company Description
Home Partners of America® is a residential real estate company that provides accessible paths to homeownership for residents across the United States. The organization focuses on offering flexible lease and home purchase options that can help individuals and households find quality homes in desirable communities. By combining real estate expertise with data-driven decision-making, Home Partners of America® aims to create long-term value for residents, investors, and partners. Team members collaborate in a mission-driven environment that values integrity, transparency, and innovation. The company supports remote work and strives to foster an inclusive, equitable workplace for professionals from diverse backgrounds.
Role Description
This is a part-time, remote Senior Data Analyst role responsible for turning complex data into clear insights that support strategic and operational decisions. The Senior Data Analyst will gather, clean, and analyze data from multiple sources, build reports and dashboards, and deliver concise findings to stakeholders across the business. Day-to-day tasks include designing and executing analyses on resident behavior, portfolio performance, and market trends, using statistical methods and data modeling techniques to identify patterns and opportunities. The person in this role will partner closely with product, operations, finance, and leadership teams to define metrics, track key performance indicators, and recommend data-informed actions. Additional responsibilities include documenting methodologies, ensuring data quality and consistency, and mentoring colleagues on best practices in analytics and data literacy.
Qualifications

Strong Analytical Skills and experience with Data Analytics, including the ability to interpret complex datasets and translate them into actionable business insights.
Solid foundation in Statistics and quantitative methods for hypothesis testing, forecasting, and performance measurement.
Hands-on experience with Data Modeling, including designing, maintaining, and optimizing data structures to support reporting and analysis.
Clear and effective Communication skills, with the ability to present findings to technical and non-technical audiences in a structured, concise manner.
Proficiency with analytics and data tools (e.g., SQL, Excel, BI platforms such as Power BI, Tableau, or Looker; experience with Python or R is a plus).
Demonstrated experience in a data-focused role, preferably in real estate, financial services, or a similar data-rich environment.
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related discipline; advanced degree is a plus.
Ability to work independently in a remote environment, manage multiple priorities, and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
Commitment to ethical use of data, attention to detail, and a continuous improvement mindset.

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
About Our Client:

The organization operates as a market research and analytics firm specializing in the disruptive economy. It addresses challenges in analyzing vast alternative data points to provide actionable insights across sectors such as Consumer Goods, AI & Software, Cloud, and Payments. Serving top investment funds and large corporations globally, the company supports high-stakes decision-making through proprietary technology and substantial data analysis. It maintains a presence in the US, APAC, and India, with a culture recognized for transparency, ownership, and continuous mastery.

About the Opportunity:

The **Data Analyst** role involves owning and managing data products within sector teams focused on industries like consumer goods, software, or financial services. The position is responsible for ensuring data accuracy, improving methodologies, and delivering trusted insights that help investors understand company performance and market trends. This role contributes by tackling complex analytical problems, collaborating cross-functionally, and continuously enhancing product value and team processes.

Responsibilities:

• Own data products end-to-end, ensuring accuracy, client value, and operational efficiency
• Develop expertise in proprietary datasets and methodologies
• Address complex data challenges with analytical judgment and creativity
• Utilize AI and automation tools to improve workflow speed and quality
• Collaborate with Research, Engineering, and other teams to align practices and unblock work
• Identify and implement improvements to products and processes

Requirements:

• 3-5+ years of experience in data analytics or related fields involving complex data
• Proficiency in SQL; experience with Python or PySpark is advantageous
• Proven ability to learn complex data methodologies and build strong mental models
• Demonstrated initiative in problem-solving and ownership beyond assigned scope
• Effective communication skills for technical and non-technical audiences
• Active use and enthusiasm for leveraging AI tools to enhance work processes

Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

Benefits & Perks:

• Flexible work hours and vacation
• Generous 401(k) match
• Parental leave
• Team events and wellness budget
• Learning reimbursement

This organization operates within the project support and coordination space, focusing on ensuring accuracy and alignment between contracts, customer projects, and estimates. It addresses challenges related to discrepancies in quantities, materials, and labor by maintaining clear communication channels between field staff, subcontractors, and retail partners. The company supports efficient project progression through detailed review and issue resolution.

The Project Coordinator is responsible for reviewing customer projects and work orders to ensure they align with contract expectations. This role involves identifying and correcting discrepancies, facilitating communication among stakeholders, and supporting the smooth advancement of projects. • Communicate effectively with field staff, subcontractors, and retail personnel

new_jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The organization operates within the energy transformation industry, addressing the challenges utilities face as they navigate complex, interconnected demands such as load flexibility, electrification, and carbon reduction. Focused on energy efficiency and clean energy solutions, this women-led firm supports utilities in managing these transitions effectively, aiming to make a measurable impact on energy practices.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Sr. Rebate Processing Specialist plays a critical role in the rebate processing operations by ensuring accurate data management, coordinating project activities, and supporting the delivery of program objectives. This position contributes to maintaining data integrity and smooth workflow processes that are essential for the successful execution of energy efficiency programs.

3. Responsibilities:
• Process rebate applications and related documentation following program guidelines and service level agreements.
• Perform timely and accurate data entry, validation, and maintenance across various systems.
• Coordinate project activities, deliverables, timelines, and documentation to support program execution.
• Review applications and documentation for accuracy, completeness, and compliance.
• Prepare and maintain reports and project documentation to support internal teams and client needs.
• Collaborate with stakeholders to resolve data discrepancies and process issues.
• Support program launches, system updates, and operational changes through testing and documentation.
• Identify and recommend process improvements for efficiency and accuracy.
• Maintain records and audit documentation per program and client standards.
• Assist with special projects, operational initiatives, and administrative tasks as needed.

4. Requirements:
• High school diploma or equivalent; associate or bachelor’s degree preferred.
• Minimum 2 years experience in data entry, project coordination, program administration, or operations support.
• Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
• Excellent organizational and time management skills.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Office, including Excel, Word, and Outlook.
• Experience with databases, CRM systems, or data management platforms preferred.
• Ability to analyze information, identify discrepancies, and resolve issues efficiently.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills.
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
• Experience in rebate processing, energy efficiency programs, utilities, or regulated environments is a plus.
• Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information appropriately.

new_jobs.title: Project Coordinator 1 (41-5004) Anchorage Therapeutic Courts
new_jobs.description:
Job Description

The Alaska Court System Therapeutic Courts, are looking to hire a Project Coordinator 1 to support both the Anchorage Felony Wellness Court and the Anchorage Municipal Wellness Court. This position is responsible for coordinating the daily operations and administrative functions of these therapeutic court programs. This position serves as a liaison between the court, treatment providers, probation, attorneys, and community partners to ensure effective communication and program coordination. This position works under the direction of the Deputy Director of Therapeutic Courts.

Employees transferring from another State of Alaska agency may receive consideration for an advance step placement.

Regular Job Duties Will Include

PROJECT ADMINISTRATION

Coordinate daily operations of the therapeutic court; meet with judges and other team members to ensure the smooth operation of the court processes;
Provide judicial officers information necessary to ensure the efficient and effective operations of court pre-meetings and court hearings;
Serve as primary contact in the court system for referrals;
Flag eligible cases, track incoming applicants/participants, monitor collection of collateral information, and track timelines of team decisions;
Process, approve or deny applications for Flexible Funds for court participants.

PROJECT CONSISTENCY

Work with the Director of Therapeutic Courts and other project coordinators to improve program effectiveness, consistency and uniformity with the goal of making it a strong and efficient part of the ongoing court/governmental system;
Work with court team to create and modify forms that will ensure consistency with court policies, procedures, and data collection needs.

PROJECT DEVELOPMENT

Work with various team members and partner agencies to update and revise policy and procedural manuals and other court documents as needed for the court;
Assist in the development of program evaluation criteria;
Work with the Director of Therapeutic Courts to develop and update training plans to support the needs of the court;
Facilitate administrative meetings which includes scheduling, preparing agendas and documents to be reviewed, taking notes, and distributing minutes.

COLLABORATION

Work on community and statewide multi-program needs (e.g., drug testing protocols, housing, vocational training and work placement) in consultation with court administrators and the Director of Therapeutic Courts;
Meet with partnering agencies to improve day-to-day operations;
Attend regular staff meetings and monthly statewide project coordinator teleconferences;
Participate in statewide planning and discussion with other project coordinators as scheduled by the Director of Therapeutic Courts.

OUTREACH AND EDUCATION

Write informational materials for public distribution;
With supervisory approval, perform community education and outreach by making regular presentations to community entities.

This position may be flexibly-staffed. An incumbent must have worked as a Project Coordinator 1 for at least two (2) years. Once that requirement is met, progression to the higher level may occur when the incumbent: 1) successfully completes a specified training plan; 2) meets all other specified terms of the flexible staffing agreement; 3) meets the minimum qualifications of the higher level; 4) is certified by the supervisor as capable to perform at the higher level; and 5) is approved by the Human Resources Department.

When certified, the position may be reclassified to Project Coordinator 2, salary range 18 ($3,171.75 bi-weekly).

This position is in the partially exempt service and the incumbent serves "at-will" to the hiring authority. This position is exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act and is not eligible for overtime compensation.

The Work Hours

Full-time court employees are currently scheduled to work 37.5 hours per week. The work hours for this position are Monday – Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. with a one-hour lunch break.

Exceptional Benefits For Full-Time Employees

We offer a competitive benefits package that includes 12 paid holidays
, medical, dental, and optional vision coverage
, and employer-paid life insurance
. Employees are enrolled in the Public Employees Retirement System (PERS)
and the Alaska Supplemental Annuity Plan (SBS)
in lieu of contributions to Social Security, with access to the Alaska Deferred Compensation Plan
. Personal leave
is accrued for vacation, illness, or personal needs.

Enjoy peace of mind and long-term security while serving your community.

See
http://doa.alaska.gov/drb/index.html
for additional information.

IMPORTANT: Please attach a letter of interest in employment as a Project Coordinator 1 with the Alaska Court System. Failure to submit a letter of interest may disqualify you from consideration for an interview.

For more information on the Alaska Court System, please visit:
http://www.courts.alaska.gov

For information about what state courts do, please watch the following short video:
VIDEO: The who, what, when, where and how of state courts

The Alaska Court System is an EEO employer and proudly promotes diversity.

Minimum Qualifications

A bachelor's degree from an accredited college AND two years of professional or administrative experience in mental health, community development, research, the behavioral sciences, the social sciences, public administration, or a closely related field.

OR

Six years of professional or administrative experience in mental health, community development, research, the behavioral sciences, the social sciences, public administration, or a closely related field.

OR

Any combination totaling six years of education from an accredited college AND professional or administrative experience in mental health, community development, research, the behavioral sciences, the social sciences, public administration, or a closely related field.

Additional Required Information

This applies to your application submission.

Documents To Be Attached To The Application (REQUIRED)

A letter of interest, completed per the instructions below.
Post-secondary transcripts, if using education to meet minimum qualifications.
A list of three (3) professional references who have had supervisory authority over you, including your most recent supervisor, along with their daytime contact phone numbers and email addresses.

LETTER OF INTEREST
Please attach a professional letter of interest to your application before submitting it online or, if unable to attach to your application, email it to recruitment@akcourts.gov
.

Your Letter Of Interest Should

Be professionally written and include proper format, grammar, spelling, and punctuation.
Explain how you meet the minimum qualifications of the position.
Express your interest in employment with the Alaska Court System.

The letter will be used to help determine which applicants will advance to the interview phase of the recruitment and selection process.

LEGAL EMPLOYMENT

The State’s online recruitment system, Workplace Alaska, requires applicants to certify that they have a legal right to accept employment in the United States. It is the responsibility of the employee to maintain the appropriate documentation to accept or continue legal employment. The Alaska Court System does not sponsor visas.

Work Experience

If using work experience not already documented in your application, provide the employer’s name, your job title, dates of employment and whether full or part-time. Applications will be reviewed to determine if the responses are supported and Minimum Qualifications are clearly met. If they are not, the applicant will not advance to the interview and selection phase of the recruitment.

EDUCATION

If post-secondary education is required to meet the Minimum Qualifications, you must fill in the Education and Training section of the application. If you have not obtained a degree, please indicate the number of credit hours earned in the Degree Obtained field. Copies of transcripts are required to verify educational credentials used to meet or support the minimum qualifications for the position and are required with each application.

SPECIAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR FOREIGN EDUCATION

Education completed in foreign colleges or universities may be used to meet the minimum qualifications listed above. If utilizing this education, you must show that the education credentials have been submitted to a private organization that specializes in the interpretation of foreign educational credentials and that such education has been deemed to be at least equivalent to that gained in conventional U.S. education programs; or an accredited U.S. post-secondary institution reports the other institution as one whose transcript is given full value, or full value is given in subject areas applicable to the curricula at the post-secondary institution. It is your responsibility to provide such evidence when applying.

SELECTION PROCEDURE

Applicants will be required to meet the Minimum Qualifications as outlined above. Unfortunately, we cannot always interview all applicants. If your application is considered "not qualified," you will be sent a notice within two weeks after the closing date for this recruitment. If you believe your application was improperly disqualified, you may appeal this decision to the Human Resources Director at 820 West 4th Avenue, Anchorage, AK 99501-2005 within five workdays from the date the notice is mailed to you. Qualified applications will be forwarded to the hiring supervisor .

MULTIPLE VACANCIES NOTICE

This recruitment may be used for more than one (1) vacancy. The applicant pool acquired during this recruitment may be used for future vacancies for up to sixty (60) days after this recruitment closes. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply to each recruitment notice to ensure consideration for all vacancies.

NEPOTISM REVIEW

To avoid a conflict of interest and the appearance of impropriety, applicants who have a spouse, close relative, and/or regular member of their household employed by the Alaska Court System may not be considered for employment without the approval of the Administrative Director .

BACKGROUND CHECK

A background check will be conducted prior to an employment offer being made. Applicants who have prior convictions that have been set aside under the terms of a suspended imposition of sentence (SIS) may answer "no" to these questions. If you are not sure if your situation requires disclosure, please contact the Human Resources Department at (907) 264-8242. The Court System reserves the right to confirm all background information.

Contact Information

Contact Title: Recruitment

E-Mail:
recruitment@akcourts.gov

Mailing Address: 820 West 4th Avenue, Anchorage, AK 99501

Phone Number: (907) 264-8242
